user_query: Update mothers teriyaki salmon bowl lunch to a tofu based dish

updated_paths: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

update_results.path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

nutrition_note.from: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.
nutrition_note.to: Tofu provides plant-based protein and is lower in saturated fat than salmon, making this a heart-friendly, vegetarian alternative. Tofu supplies calcium and iron (especially if fortified); broccoli adds fiber and vitamin C; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and added sugars—use low-sodium soy sauce and moderate sweetener to control levels.

short_description.from: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
short_description.to: Crispy pan-fried tofu glazed with a homemade teriyaki sauce, served over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger — a satisfying tofu-based alternative to the salmon bowl.

meal_suggestion.from: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
meal_suggestion.to: Press extra-firm tofu to remove excess moisture, then cut into thick slices or cubes. Lightly dust with cornstarch and pan-fry in a nonstick skillet with a small amount of oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Prepare a teriyaki glaze by simmering low-sodium soy sauce, mirin (or sake + a little sugar), grated ginger, and a touch of rice vinegar until slightly thickened. Toss the fried tofu in the glaze to coat. Serve the glazed tofu over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori; optionally add sliced scallions or a few steamed edamame for extra protein.
